No history yet — waiting for first cycle
MACRO SCORE — CROSS-POD RESEARCH SIGNALS
How this works
All pods blend three independent macro signals into a single macro score:
1. Polymarket Sentiment (50%) — volume-weighted confidence from prediction markets, scaled to [-1, +1].
2. FRED Score (30%) — composite of yield curve slope, VIX stress, and credit spread from the Federal Reserve.
3. News Score (20%) — average sentiment polarity from financial news headlines across all asset classes (LLM-scored for relevancy and impact, with keyword fallback).

macro_score = 0.5 × poly_sentiment + 0.3 × fred_score + 0.2 × news_score

Score > 0 = risk-on environment. Near 0 = uncertainty. Score < 0 = risk-off.
Polymarket Sentiment
—
50% weight
+
FRED Score
—
30% weight
+
News Score
—
20% weight
→
Blended Macro Score
—

FRED Composite Score —
-1.0 Risk-Off 0 Neutral +1.0 Risk-On
23 series across rates, inflation, volatility, labor, growth, commodities, and liquidity. Composite score derived from yield curve (T10Y2Y), VIX (VIXCLS), and credit spread (BAMLH0A0HYM2). Cached hourly. Score carries 30% weight in the blended macro_score.
